name: Salmon Sauteed with Zinfandel Butter
recipeIngredient: 3/4 lb Salmon Fillet, skinless and boneless, cut into 2 pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt 1 tablespoon of butter in a 10-12 inch frying pan over medium-high heat.
2. Add mushrooms and cook, stirring often, until they are lightly browned and liquid has evaporated, about 6-9 minutes. Remove from the pan and set aside.
3. Add another tablespoon of butter to the pan and swirl until melted.
4. Add salmon and cook, turning once, until fish is just slightly translucent or wet in the thickest part (test by cutting), about 7-10 minutes total. Remove from the pan and keep warm.
5. In a separate bowl, combine shallots and Zinfandel wine. Bring to a boil over high heat; continue to boil, uncovered, until the mixture is reduced to about 1/2 cup, about 4 minutes.
6. Reduce heat to low. Add the remaining 2 tablespoons of butter in one chunk and heat, stirring, until butter is melted and sauce is smooth.
7. Place salmon on two warm dinner plates; top with mushrooms and chives. Spoon sauce over and around the salmon.
Per Serving: Calories: 472, Protein: 36 g, Fat: 34 g, Carbohydrate: 5.6 g, Sodium: 317 mg, Cholesterol: 156 mg.
